sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Follow-up of chronic problems and discussion of current depression and hypertension management.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports doing well over the past six months with depression management, utilizing counseling and regular swimming. He continues to take his hypertension medication, norvasc, and checks his blood pressure at home approximately once a week. He reports occasional salt intake, which can lead to ankle swelling, primarily in the evenings. He has completed a review of systems sheet and reports no current nasal congestion, chest pain, or shortness of breath. He is managing his rotator cuff repair with home exercises.
Findings
The patient's blood pressure measured at 156/94 mmHg today. A slight three out of six systolic ejection murmur was noted on heart exam. Lung exam was clear. Lower extremity exam revealed 1-2+ pitting edema in the ankles. Laboratory results show stable creatinine and no other abnormalities. An EKG demonstrates no evidence of coronary artery disease.
Assessment
The patient's primary problem is depression, which is currently well-managed. His hypertension is not optimally controlled. He has a stable rotator cuff repair.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: Increase norvasc to 10 mg daily. Order an echocardiogram and lipid panel. Continue home blood pressure monitoring. Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to assess progress and adjust medication as needed.
